preview_text:
The model rates Saudi Arabia as the slight favorite with a 40% win probability, while Cape Verde sits at 28%, giving the underdog a meaningful deficit in direct matchup odds. The gap reflects a modest edge rather than overwhelming dominance; both teams enter with realistic paths to victory.

The 31% draw probability from the model suggests this match has genuine potential to end level. The three outcomes cluster within a 12-point band around the Saudi Arabia win, indicating a reasonably competitive fixture without a heavily skewed outcome distribution. A stalemate remains plausible enough to merit consideration in both teams' scenarios.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge noticeably on draw likelihood. The market strips that to 26%, roughly 5 percentage points below the model estimate, while ranking Saudi Arabia and Cape Verde nearly identically at 39% and 34% respectively. Polymarket pricing aligns closely with the model on the Saudi Arabia win (40%) but also shows lower draw odds (26%). The dashboard offers no view on which assessment of draw probability is more accurate.
